_doc: BIG real-repo multi-language corpora — the point is to test whether ctx-optimize's speed/footprint edge HOLDS AT SCALE across languages, and whether rival tools choke. Clone --depth 1 at ref into the arena (NOT the repo tree). Re-count actual files per run and print next to every number. If a (tool,corpus) cell exceeds ~10 min or OOMs, record a TIMEOUT/FAIL cell (that is itself a scale finding) and move on — never fake.

corpora.note: The canonical BIG Go repo. Use pkg/ (still huge, ~9k files); full repo is ~15k+ and vendored. If a tool cannot finish pkg/ in 10 min, record timeout — that is a scale finding.

scale_variant.warning: ~15k+ files incl vendor — opt-in extreme test only

corpora.note: The canonical BIG Java repo (all modules).
Big real C# codebase (Entity Framework Core).
The TypeScript compiler itself — big TS with very large files.

_optional_small_controls: flask/gin/gson/hono/Newtonsoft.Json from the prior small manifest can be re-added as fast sanity controls, but the primary bench is the big repos above.
